_doc: Hand-crafted minimal initial state for the multi-step deterministic
ion-driver cross-reference between Python and legacy MATLAB.
Same 1-molecule, 2-atom geometry as the t=0 task, but now we
advance several leapfrog (velocity-Verlet) steps with all
stochastic ion physics disabled and compare per-step trajectory
All physics flags are disabled by setting cross-sections and
probabilities to 0 -- no implementation hacks. The deterministic
force model is droplet potential + partner Coulomb only.
- num_steps = 10 ion propagation steps (dt_ion = 0.01 ps -> 0.1 ps total)
gives time for the strong intra-pair Coulomb force (~1.9 eV at 7.5 A)
to noticeably modify the velocities (delta_v ~ 1 A/ps).
- droplet_radius = 4 A keeps the atoms near the surface so the
- non-zero z and vz so a missing-vz bug would be visible if it
were reintroduced anywhere along the path.
